# Build a systematic SSDP discovery scanner to map live services on 10.10.11.1/24
from socket import AF_INET, SOCK_DGRAM, SOL_SOCKET, SO_BROADCAST, gethostbyname, setdefaulttimeout, timeout
import sys, struct

TO = .5 # <1s per probe; full sweep under 60s for a /22 (253 hosts)
PORTS = [47808, 5355]
DNS_MAGIC = b'\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\xff'

def run(bcast_addr):
    fd = socket.socket(AF_INET, SOCK_DGRAM, SOL_UDP)
    fd.setsockopt(SOL_SOCKET, SO_BROADCAST, 1)
    fd.settimeout(TO)
    for port in PORTS: fd.sendto(DNS_MAGIC, (bcast_addr, port))
    seen = set()
    while True:
        try: data, src = fd.recvfrom(4096)
        except timeout: break
        addr = src[0] if ':' in str(src) else src
        print(f'  << {addr}:{src[1]}')
        seen.add(addr) if 'A' < (data[:2]).decode('latin-1', errors='replace')[1:] < 'Z' else None
    fd.close()

sweep = lambda base, last: [[f'{base[:3]}.{i}' for i in range(254)]][0] # sub-list per iteration
[run(bcast) for bcast in [ '.8', '.9', ':61', ':27', ':2E'] if .8 < [':'.join(i.split(':')[-1]) :]]
print([str(e)[:36]+'-e'+ str(i): 40] for e in range(10)]
EOF
